sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Merck & Co., Inc.는 2026-03-31 분기에 매출 162억 8,600만 달러를 기록했습니다.

keyPoints.text: Merck는 2026년 3월 Terns와 주당 $53 가격으로 총 약 6.7 billion 인수계약을 체결해 TERN-701(Philadelphia chromosome-positive, chronic phase CML 대상 Phase 1/2 후보)을 확보했다.
Keytruda/Keytruda Qlex 매출은 1분기 2026년에 12% 성장했고, 미국은 약 $250 million 유리요인(도매상 구매 시점·순가격·수요 증가)과 적응증 확대로 받쳐졌다.
영업활동은 3.9 billion을 창출한 반면 투자활동은 10.2 billion을 사용해, 현금창출과 자본지출 동반 확장이 동시에 진행된 분기였다.

riskChanges.text: SG&A는 6% 늘었고, 핵심 원인으로 행정비용 확대와 외환의 불리한 영향이 제시돼 비용구조 리스크가 커졌다.
현금 및 투자자산이 15,521에서 6,807로 내려 유동성 여력이 줄어든 점은 단기 충격 흡수 여지를 낮출 수 있다.
대규모 사업개발은 영업활동 현금과 부채 조합으로 조달될 수 있다고 해, 추가 M&A가 확대되면 조달구조 변화와 차입 노출이 커질 수 있다.

businessChanges.text: Lynparza는 341과 312, 그리고 9 %가 함께 제시돼 해당 라인업의 수치 변화가 뚜렷했다.
Welireg는 199와 137, 45 %가 병기돼 Lynparza와 다른 수요 경로가 존재함을 보여준다.
영업비용 구조는 SG&A가 6% 증가했고, 관리비 상승과 환율 악영향이 함께 비용 압력 요인으로 작동했다.

financialHighlights.text: 매출은 162억 8,600만 달러였으나 순이익은 -42억 4,000만 달러로 분기 실적은 적자다.
총자산은 1,286억 8,500만 달러, 현금및현금성자산은 53억 2,700만 달러로 분기 말 재무규모와 현금 여력을 보여준다.

watchItems.text: 12% 성장이 약 $250 million의 가격·구매 타이밍 요소와 조기/전이성 적응증 수요에 크게 기대고 있어, 동일한 수요·가격 메커니즘이 유지되는지 적응증별 추적이 핵심이다.
순이익 -42억 4,000만 달러와 투자활동 10.2 billion 사용이 맞물릴 때, 6.7 billion급 인수 논의가 지속되면 영업현금 외 조달 조합 변화가 재무 부담으로 전이될 수 있다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2026-03-31 revenue = 162억 8,600만 달러 (USD 16.286B; 16,286 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 net income = -42억 4,000만 달러 (USD -4.24B; -4,240 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 total assets = 1,286억 8,500만 달러 (USD 128.685B; 128,685 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 cash and cash equivalents = 53억 2,700만 달러 (USD 5.327B; 5,327 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations Business Development Transactions Below is a summary of significant business development activity thus far in 2026. In March 2026, Merck entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Terns), a clinical-stage oncology company, for $53 per share, for a total transaction value of approximately $6.7 billion. Through this acquisition, Merck will acquire Terns’ lead candidate, TERN-701, a novel investigational oral allosteric BCR::ABL1 tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) currently being evaluated in a Phase 1/2 trial for patients with Philadelphia chromosome-positive, chronic phase chronic myeloid leukemia previously treated with at least one prior TKI and who experienced treatment failure, suboptimal response or treatment intolerance. Results of Operations Business Development Transactions Below is a summary of significant business development activity thus far in 2026. In March 2026, Merck entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Terns Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Terns), a clinical-stage oncology company, for $53 per share, for a total transaction value of approximately $6.7 billion. Through this acquisition, Merck will acquire Terns’ lead candidate, TERN-701, a novel investigational oral allosteric BCR::ABL1 tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) currently being evaluated in a Phase 1/2 trial for patients with Philadelphia chromosome-positive, chronic phase chronic myeloid leukemia previously treated with at least one prior TKI and who experienced treatment failure, suboptimal response or treatment intolerance. The transaction has been approved by both Merck’s and Terns’ Boards of Directors. The acquisition is subject to

Research and Development Update” below. Combined global sales of Keytruda/Keytruda Qlex grew 12% in the first quarter of 2026. Sales growth in the U.S. reflects an approximate $250 million favorable impact due to the timing of wholesaler purchases, higher net pricing, and increased demand. Demand in the U.S. was driven by higher utilization across earlier-stage indications, including in certain types of cervical cancer, triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), and renal cell carcinoma (RCC), as well as higher demand across multiple metastatic indications, in particular for the treatment of certain types of urothelial and cervical cancers. Sales growth in international markets reflects higher demand in urothelial, non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), gastric, cervical, and endometrial cancer metastatic indications, as well as increased uptake predominately for the TNBC, NSCLC, melanoma, and R

General and Administrative Selling, general and administrative (SG&A) expenses increased 6% in the first quarter of 2026 primarily due to higher administrative costs and the unfavorable impact of foreign exchange. - 35 -

Liquidity and Capital Resources ($ in millions) March 31, 2026 December 31, 2025 Cash and investments $ 6,807 $ 15,521 Working capital 8,070 15,189 Total debt to total liabilities and equity 38.2 % 36.0 % Cash provided by operating activities was $3.9 billion in the first three months of 2026 compared with $2.5 billion in the first three months of 2025. Cash provided by operating activities continues to be the Company’s primary source of funds to finance operating needs, with excess cash serving as the primary source of funds to finance business development transactions, capital expenditures, dividends paid to shareholders and treasury stock purchases. Larger business development transactions may be funded with a combination of cash from operating activities and debt.
